title: Implement entity handles and foundational component stores
summary: Create generation-checked entity handles, bounded component stores, stable iteration and deferred structural command application with invariant tests.
acceptance: Destroyed entity handles cannot alias newly allocated entities
Iteration and conflict resolution use documented stable ordering
Structural changes are deferred to explicit commit phases
Property/invariant tests cover allocation, destruction, reuse and capacity boundaries

history.details: Implemented generation-checked EntityId registry, bounded Int32 component stores, deterministic structural command commit phase, invariant tests, and entity-store benchmark; required checks passed.

changes_requested: High: stale destroyed handles can still read components through Int32ComponentStore.read/has when EntityRegistry.destroy is used directly; Medium: StructuralCommandBuffer silently wraps out-of-range Int32 command values before commit; Medium: iteration/commit APIs allocate proportional entity/result objects in sim-core hot paths.

Addressed reviewer findings: registry-backed component read/has validation, queue-time Int32 bounds checks, primitive iteration callbacks, reusable typed commit result buffers, and line-count/report corrections; owner gates passed.

review.summary: High: stale destroyed handles can still read components through Int32ComponentStore.read/has when EntityRegistry.destroy is used directly; Medium: StructuralCommandBuffer silently wraps out-of-range Int32 command values before commit; Medium: iteration/commit APIs allocate proportional entity/result objects in sim-core hot paths.
